message:
feat(speculative): implement self-speculative drafting with VIP pinning

Replace separate Q2K draft model with self-speculative approach:
- Use same Q4K model for both drafting and verification
- Pin VIP experts (0, 4) for all 32 layers (~5.3GB RAM)
- Draft phase: forced VIP routing → RAM speed (200 GB/s)
- Verify phase: real router → 80% RAM hits, 20% SSD

Key changes:
- Remove draft_engine field and initDraftModel()
- Add enableSelfSpeculativeDrafting() with preloadAllVIPExperts()
- Add draftSelfSpeculative() using main model with VIP experts
- Update speculativeDecodePLD() to use self-speculative tier 2
- Remove --draft CLI argument (no longer needed)

Memory budget fits in 16GB:
- Attention: 2.6GB + VIPs: 5.3GB + KV: 0.5GB = ~10.4GB

This eliminates OOM when loading Q4K + Q2K models simultaneously.

files.patch:
@@ -265,6 +265,10 @@ pub const MetalMoEEngine = struct {
     /// Cold experts: fall back to Q2K (smaller, fits in RAM)
     /// This achieves Q4K quality for ~80% of routing while fitting in 16GB
     expert_level_hybrid_mode: bool,
+    /// VIP-Only mode: Draft engine uses ONLY experts 0 and 4 (hardcoded)
+    /// This reduces the draft model from 14GB → 3.5GB, fitting 100% in RAM
+    /// The VIP experts are "generalists" that handle grammar and structure
+    vip_only_mode: bool,
     // CSR outlier buffers per expert per layer (768 total = 32 layers × 8 experts × 3 projections)
     // Indexed as: hybrid_csr_gate[layer * 8 + expert]
     hybrid_csr_gate_row_ptr: [256]?objc.MTLBuffer, // [n_ff + 1] per tensor
@@ -605,6 +609,9 @@ pub const MetalMoEEngine = struct {
             .hybrid_mixed_mode = false,
             .pure_q2k_file_mode = false,
             .expert_level_hybrid_mode = false,
+
+            // VIP-Only Mode: Draft engine uses only experts 0 and 4
+            .vip_only_mode = false,
             .hybrid_csr_gate_row_ptr = [_]?objc.MTLBuffer{null} ** 256,
             .hybrid_csr_gate_col_idx = [_]?objc.MTLBuffer{null} ** 256,
             .hybrid_csr_gate_values = [_]?objc.MTLBuffer{null} ** 256,
